channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Apple reveals new AI architecture built around Google Gemini models
item.description: unclefuzzy: Apple has announced a new AI architecture designed around Google Gemini models. This represents a strategic choice by Apple to build core AI capabilities on top of Google's foundation models rather than developing proprietary alternatives.

item.title: Apple Core AI Framework
item.description: hmokiguess: Apple Core AI Framework is an AI development framework from Apple. Details about its specific capabilities and use cases are limited in the provided text.

item.title: Microsoft Hacked to Deliver Malware to Claude and Gemini Users
item.description: guessmyname: A Hacker News post reports that Microsoft was compromised to deliver malware targeting Claude and Gemini users. This describes a purported supply-chain security incident involving major cloud platforms and AI assistants.

item.title: Ask HN: What are tools you have made for yourself since the advent of AI?
item.description: aryamaan: A Hacker News discussion thread where users share custom tools they have built for themselves in response to AI capabilities. The thread invites community members to discuss their personal AI-assisted tool creation.

item.title: Train Models Faster with JAX and MaxText Using NVFP4 on NVIDIA Blackwell
item.description: NVIDIA Developer: NVIDIA published guidance on training models faster using JAX and MaxText with NVFP4 quantization on NVIDIA Blackwell accelerators. The focus is on improving throughput for pre-training frontier large language models across distributed hardware.

item.title: Siri AI
item.description: 0xedb: A Hacker News post by 0xedb discussing Siri's AI capabilities and system. The brief title suggests analysis or opinion about Apple's voice assistant technology.

item.title: Nex N2 Pro: Frontier agentic performance at 400B
item.description: himata4113: Nex N2 Pro is a 400 billion parameter language model designed for agentic performance. It represents frontier-level capabilities in autonomous agent applications.

item.title: HN seems dead compared to say 10-15 years ago
item.description: morpheos137: The author argues that Hacker News community discourse has declined in quality over 10-15 years, with fewer original ideas, excessive nostalgia posts, and widespread LLM cargo culting. They question whether perpetual growth can continue as fundamental physics limits and energy constraints tighten.

item.title: We need to learn how to argue with AI
item.description: uxhacker: The author argues that humans should develop skills to engage in argumentation with AI systems. This commentary advocates for learning how to effectively debate or reason through disagreements with AI, a skill the author suggests will become increasingly important.

item.title: Show HN: Gitdot – a better GitHub. Open-source, anti-AI, and written in Rust
item.description: baepaul: Gitdot is an open-source GitHub alternative written in Rust with a CLI-inspired web interface. It currently supports user signups, organization creation, public and private repositories, and GitHub repository imports. The platform emphasizes keyboard-driven navigation and targets 100ms First Contentful Paint performance.

item.title: Show HN: Startup sci-fi novel that took me 5 years to write
item.description: mck-: A startup founder shares his 5-year journey writing "Blockchained," a sci-fi thriller about a startup founder meeting a mysterious investor. The post reflects on creative flow, audience awareness, literary craft refinement, and the psychology of shipping creative work.

item.title: Two Leaps to 1000 Tokens/s on a 1T-Parameter Model
item.description: __natty__: The source discusses approaches to reach 1000 tokens per second throughput on a trillion-parameter model. This focuses on inference optimization techniques for scaling very large models to practical production speeds.

item.title: Simulating Percolation in Python: How Do Wildfires & Diseases Spread?
item.description: NeuralNine: This video tutorial demonstrates how to implement percolation simulations in Python to model the spread of wildfires and diseases. Percolation theory is used to understand how phenomena propagate through networks and systems.

item.title: AI is slowing down
item.description: crescit_eundo: The post titled "AI is slowing down" presents a viewpoint that AI development or progress is decelerating. This is a commentary on the current trajectory of artificial intelligence advancement.

item.title: How Confident Are AI Classifiers About Their Own Confidence?
item.description: apwheele: An inquiry into whether AI classifiers accurately calibrate their confidence scores and know the limits of their own certainty. The post examines whether models can reliably assess their own prediction quality.

item.title: Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math
item.description: Tomte: Terence Tao, a prominent mathematician, has become an advocate for AI tools in mathematical research. The report from HackerNews covers his evangelism and position on AI's role in mathematics.

item.title: MiMo-v2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed: 1T model with 1000 tokens per second
item.description: gainsurier: MiMo-v2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed is a 1 trillion parameter language model optimized for high-speed inference, achieving 1000 tokens per second. This appears to be a variant or release of the MiMo model line focused on throughput performance.

item.title: Hermes Agent is crazy… 180,000+ github stars
item.description: David Ondrej: Hermes Agent is an open-source agent framework with over 180,000 GitHub stars. The video demonstrates running Hermes Agent with MiniMax m3 model for cost optimization, claiming 100x cheaper inference.

item.title: "Chat is dead": OpenAI preps overhaul of ChatGPT
item.description: kylemaxwell: OpenAI is preparing a significant overhaul of ChatGPT. The title suggests a shift away from the traditional chat interface paradigm.

item.title: ultracode is the most powerful claude code feature in months
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI discusses Ultracode as a significant new feature addition to Claude's code capabilities. The feature is characterized as the most powerful code feature released in recent months.

item.title: Launch HN: Intuned (YC S22) – Build and run reliable browser automations as code
item.description: fkilaiwi: Intuned is a platform for building and deploying browser automations as code, with AI agents that can automatically generate automations and heal code when websites change. The platform provides managed infrastructure for running Playwright-based TypeScript or Python projects, handling scheduling, observability, and maintenance through an integrated agent system.

item.title: Why The Universe Ignores Most People
item.description: David Ondrej: A YouTube video by David Ondrej with a philosophical title suggesting commentary on why some people remain unnoticed or insignificant in the world. The exact argument or claims are not detailed in the provided metadata.

item.title: Import AI 460: Reward hacking society, RSI data from Anthropic; and RL-based quadcopter racing
item.description: Import AI: The newsletter poses a question about whether and when financial markets will incorporate expectations about a technological singularity into their pricing mechanisms.

item.title: Replies to comments on my "LLMs are eroding my career" post
item.description: omblivion: A discussion thread responding to claims that large language models are eroding career prospects. The post engages with comments around LLM adoption and its effects on knowledge workers and professional development.

item.title: Start a 1-Person AI Creative Agency with Higgsfield + Claude
item.description: Liam Ottley: Liam Ottley demonstrates how to build a one-person AI creative agency using Higgsfield for image generation, Claude for automation and copywriting, Notion for client databases, and Apify for competitor scraping. The system produces product photography, UGC ads, commercials, and ad copy while automatically tracking performance and competitor data.

item.title: Do agents.md files help coding agents?
item.description: smushback: The post discusses whether agents.md files are helpful for coding agents. It references a Twitter discussion and a recent research paper on the topic, raising questions about how agents can be guided or constrained through documentation files.

item.title: ultracode is INSANE and nobody is talking about it
item.description: Chase AI: A video discusses ultracode as a notable tool for code generation or development. The creator emphasizes that ultracode warrants more attention in the AI community.

item.title: The Most Powerful Claude Code Feature In Months Dropped & Nobody is Talking About It
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI discusses Graphify, a knowledge graph tool integrated with Claude Code that addresses memory constraints when processing large codebases. Graphify enables more efficient, token-light responses by constructing knowledge graphs without relying on RAG, and covers Claude Code features including Ultracode and Dynamic Workflows.

item.title: DeepSeek V4 Pro beats GPT-5.5 Pro on precision
item.description: yogthos: DeepSeek V4 Pro outperforms GPT-5.5 Pro on precision tasks according to this comparison. The claim suggests DeepSeek's model achieves better precision in specific evaluations.

item.title: Built to benefit everyone: our plan
item.description: OpenAI: OpenAI articulates its vision for AI focusing on three pillars: access to AI benefits, safety in development, and ensuring shared prosperity as the organization works toward AGI. The statement emphasizes the importance of making AI benefits available broadly rather than concentrated among few.

item.title: The Open Source Community is backing OpenEnv for Agentic RL
item.description: Hugging Face: The open source community is supporting OpenEnv, a framework for agentic reinforcement learning. This represents growing momentum behind open source infrastructure for RL-based agents.

item.title: Introducing the OpenAI Economic Research Exchange
item.description: OpenAI: OpenAI launched the Economic Research Exchange to fund and coordinate selected research projects studying AI's impact on employment, productivity, and broader economic effects. Applications are open for participating research initiatives.

item.title: datasette-agent-edit 0.1a0
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison released datasette-agent-edit 0.1a0, a base plugin for Datasette Agent that provides reusable tools for agentic text editing. The plugin implements view, str_replace, and insert tools modeled after Claude's text editor design, enabling collaborative editing of Markdown, SQL queries, and SVG files.

item.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch AI raises concerns about potential token price increases as major AI companies prepare for public markets. The piece frames upcoming IPOs as a driver of cost pressures in the AI sector.

item.title: Show HN: Nightwatch, The open-source, read-only AI SRE
item.description: egorferber: Nightwatch is an open-source, read-only monitoring layer that groups alert storms into incidents and deploys an AI agent to investigate live systems. It uses a local-first architecture with distributed agents in each environment sending data to a central brain, and can leverage local or remote LLMs for tool-calling while masking sensitive data before remote API calls.

item.title: Billions spent and hypothetical returns: the AI boom explained with six charts
item.description: billybuckwheat: A Hacker News post presenting six charts to explain the AI industry boom, focusing on capital expenditure and hypothetical returns from AI investments. The analysis examines spending levels and forward-looking projections for AI development.

item.title: Amazing Digital Dentures (a failed project)
item.description: Hugging Face: Hugging Face published a post-mortem analysis of the Amazing Digital Dentures project, which did not succeed. The post examines what went wrong in this initiative.

item.title: Social intelligence Arises Between Minds - Psychology Today
item.description: Google News AI: An article from Psychology Today explores how social intelligence develops and emerges through interaction between individuals. The piece examines the relational dynamics underlying human social cognition.

item.title: Ask HN: What's your favorite HN Recap like podcast?
item.description: randomor: A long-time listener of Wondercraft's HN Recap podcast finds the format too rigid and repetitive, with summaries that sometimes miss the main point. The author notes that current AI models should enable better synthesis and asks the community for podcast recommendations that better capture interesting, non-mainstream tech stories.

item.title: Notion restores access to Anthropic after service disruption
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Notion experienced a service disruption that disrupted access to Anthropic. Notion's head of product expressed surprise at the social media attention the incident received.

item.title: OpenAI is still working on that ‘super app’
item.description: TechCrunch AI: OpenAI is developing a "super app" and a senior employee claims that chat as a dominant interface is ending. The company continues work on new application paradigms beyond conversational chat.

item.title: The OnlyFans Economy of American AI
item.description: futurisold: A Hacker News post by futurisold proposing a comparison between the contemporary American AI industry and OnlyFans' creator-monetization model. The characterization suggests a viewpoint on how AI economics function or should function in the current landscape.

item.title: The ROI of AI coding looks different when you are a bootstrapped founder
item.description: tavioto: The return on investment from AI-assisted coding differs depending on whether a founder is bootstrapped or externally funded. Cost-benefit tradeoffs of AI tools appear distinct for founders without venture capital backing.

item.title: Will artificial intelligence soon escape human control? - The Economist
item.description: Google News AI: The Economist explores the question of whether artificial intelligence could soon escape human control. The article presents arguments and concerns about AI systems potentially operating beyond human oversight or governance.

item.title: Anthropic, please ship an official Claude Desktop for Linux
item.description: predkambrij: The author requests that Anthropic release an official desktop application for Claude on the Linux operating system. Currently Claude Desktop appears to lack native Linux support despite availability on other platforms.

item.title: LLMs are eroding my software engineering career and I don't know what to do
item.description: poisonfountain: A software engineer expresses concern that LLMs are eroding their career prospects and seeks advice on how to adapt. The post reflects broader anxieties in the engineering community about skill devaluation and job displacement due to AI capabilities.

item.title: Ask HN: How are thinking efforts implemented?
item.description: simianwords: Thinking efforts are a tunable parameter in Claude and ChatGPT that control the amount of inference-time computation allowed, typically set to levels like low, medium, high, and extra-high. The implementation may involve different models or parameter configurations, with evidence from cache-breaking behavior when changing effort mid-conversation in Claude.

item.title: How Twitter Rewired My Brain
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej presents a personal essay on how Twitter has affected his cognitive patterns and brain function. The title suggests a reflective take on social media's influence on neural and behavioral rewiring.

item.title: Show HN: Lathe – Use LLMs to learn a new domain, not skip past it
item.description: devenjarvis: Lathe is a tool that uses LLMs to generate hands-on, source-backed tutorials for technical topics. Users work through tutorials locally in a browser UI by reading and typing code by hand, with features like tables of contents, side-notes, exercises, and the ability to verify content compiles and ask clarifying questions.

item.title: Billions spent and hypothetical returns: the AI boom explained with six charts - The Guardian
item.description: Google News AI: The Guardian article examines the substantial capital investments in AI and questions whether financial returns justify the billions being spent. It uses six charts to explain the current state of the AI boom and its economic implications.
